hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (Y, F, W) will improve SSTR2 selectivity by forming specific π-π stacking interactions with ECL2 residue 193 (SSTR2-unique), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will enhance TM6 (residue 276) complementarity and reduce off-target binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions. Iteration 15 confirms this hypothesis but highlights the need for more aggressive mutation strategies at these positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: Target non-pharmacophore positions that are likely involved in off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) interactions. Disrupting these interactions can improve SSTR2 selectivity.
Propose mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to break conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.
